mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following a rate decline, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, labor market deterioration signals imminent corporate earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate collapsing consumer demand across durable and non-durable sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 50% in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to correlate with positive 20-day SPX returns during recovery phases, yielding a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Lower consumer price index readings reduce inflation expectations and compress real discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL declines are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in >55% of observations, or the predictive IC remains below 0.02 across rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales falling below 400,000 million dollars signals severe consumer spending weakness that forces corporate earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in >60% of periods where RSAFS drops below 400,000, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining total employment and rising unemployment indicates broad labor market deterioration that suppresses aggregate income and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with positive returns occurring in >50% of co-occurring decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP and housing starts during a monetary easing cycle reflect synchronized demand expansion that boosts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 and HOUST increases during easing cycles fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns, with negative returns exceeding 50% of observations.
